mnemonics.title: DBMS Environment Components
mnemonics.content: Remember as 'HSPD-U': Hardware, Software, Procedures, Data, Users - the five pillars of database environment

mnemonics.title: Three Schema Levels
mnemonics.content: Think 'ECI': External → Conceptual → Internal - layers from user view to storage

mnemonics.title: ACID Properties
mnemonics.content: A.C.I.D. = Atomic transactions Conserve Integrity Despite disasters - remember each letter's meaning

acronyms.usage: Software that enables database creation, maintenance, and manipulation
Person responsible for managing the database system
Standard language for managing relational databases
Four properties that guarantee reliable transaction processing
Commands for defining database structure (CREATE, ALTER, DROP)
Commands for data operations (SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE)

visualTips.visualization: Picture a pyramid: Top (External - user view) is smallest and most specific, Middle (Conceptual - logical) is the bridge, Bottom (Internal - physical) is the largest foundation. Arrows show data flow upward and independence arrows pointing downward.
Think of schema as an empty form/template (the structure), and instance as the filled form (actual data). Schema is static like a blueprint, instance changes constantly.
Draw a layered cake: Top layer = User interface, Middle = Query processor + Transaction manager, Bottom = Storage manager + Buffer manager, Base = Physical disk.
Imagine a building: Physical changes = renovating rooms (internal), Logical changes = changing floor plan (conceptual). The external view (what visitors see) stays unaffected.

analogies.analogy: Like a building blueprint - defines structure but not the actual contents. The blueprint tells you where walls and rooms are, just as schema defines tables and relationships.
Like a library system: Librarian (DBA) manages books (data), catalog (metadata) shows location, patrons (users) borrow books following rules (procedures), shelves (storage) hold physical books.
Like a photograph vs the camera settings - the settings (schema) stay the same while each photo (instance) captures different data at that moment.

Transferring money: Atomicity = entire transfer or nothing, Consistency = account balances must balance, Isolation = other transactions don't see intermediate state, Durability = once confirmed, record persists.
